selected.reason: Opening slide with personal narrative and core theme, sets course context and establishes speaker's background.
Clear slide listing three core reasons for using HTML, with distinct visual sections and concise text — key teaching artifact.
Demonstrates design concept with visual metaphor ('Build the Thing First') and code snippet, useful for explaining design-then-code workflow.
Slide with actionable list for preventing design slop, includes bullet points and UI components — practical teaching content.
Screenshot of a real web tool (X.Bookmark Resurfacer) with interface elements, demonstrates practical application mentioned in lecture.
Slide with interactive prompt and arrow navigating text — highlights key decision-making in design process, useful for UX explanation.
Slide with three pillars of 'BUILD SOMETHING SMALL' — clear, structured teaching artifact for project methodology.
Important conclusion slide summarizing the evolution of coding as a skill with quote — strong takeaway for course distillation.

dropped_summary: Dropped frames included lecturer-only shots, blank or low-information frames, slides with minimal text, duplicated content, or UI states without key teaching elements. Many frames showed intermediate states, browser navigation, or introductory material without novel content.
